      "question": "Consider the unconstrained minimization problem of a non-convex function f(x) = -x^3 - 2x^2 + x + 1. What is the condition under which the gradient descent method will converge to a critical point, and what is the nature of this critical point?",

      "marks": 15,
      "hint": "The gradient descent method for a convex function will always converge to a local minimum, but for a non-convex function, the convergence is not g
